request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The intervention, biological system, comparator, and measurable outcome are specified, but the success criterion and mechanistic rationale are missing.
clarifying_questions: What is the numeric success threshold (e.g. >=15% improvement)?
What is the mechanistic rationale for using trehalose in this context?

hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

references.title: Ethylene Glycol and Trehalose Cryopreservation of Cell-Laden Hydrogel Microspheres Enabled by Microfluidic Fabrication.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the use of trehalose in cryopreservation, which is directly relevant to the proposed hypothesis of adding trehalose to a freezing medium.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of adding trehalose to a freezing medium is covered in paper [4], which discusses the use of trehalose in cryopreservation, although it focuses on cell-laden hydrogel microspheres rather than HeLa cells specifically. The biological system of HeLa cells is not directly addressed in the retrieved papers, but the general context of cryopreservation is present. The experiment type of comparative cryopreservation is also relevant as it relates to the assessment of post-thaw viability, which is a common outcome in cryopreservation studies.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved in freezing medium containing 250 mM trehalose versus DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved in freezing medium with 250 mM trehalose, and the other group will be cryopreserved in DMSO. After thawing, cells will be allowed to recover for 30 minutes at 37°C before viability assessment using trypan blue exclusion method. Each condition will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved in DMSO (10% v/v) as a standard cryoprotectant.
HeLa cells without any cryoprotectant, frozen in medium only (control for cryopreservation effects).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare freezing medium by mixing 90% FBS (v/v) with 10% DMSO (v/v) for the control group. For the trehalose group, prepare freezing medium by mixing 90% FBS (v/v) with 10% DMSO (v/v) and adding 250 mM trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531) to the solution. Filter sterilize both solutions using a 0.22 µm filter.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL conical tubes, 0.22 µm filter, Pipettes and tips
Reagents: 90% FBS (v/v), 10% DMSO (v/v), 250 mM trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531)
Expected outcome: Both freezing media are prepared and sterile filtered, ready for use in cell cryopreservation.
Safety: DMSO is a skin irritant; wear gloves and goggles.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until they reach 70-80% confluence (approximately 24 h).
Equipment: T75 culture flasks, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), 10% FBS (v/v), 1% penicillin-streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 70-80% confluence, ready for harvesting.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize trypsin with an equal volume of complete DMEM. Centrifuge cells at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature, and resuspend in appropriate freezing medium (either trehalose or DMSO).
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), Pipettes and tips
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), Complete DMEM (with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin)
Expected outcome: HeLa cells are detached, centrifuged, and resuspended in freezing medium.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of HeLa cell suspension (1 × 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials (Nunc, 368632). Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Thermo Scientific, Revco) set to cool at -1°C/min until reaching -80°C, then transfer to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer (Thermo Scientific, Revco), Cryovials (Nunc, 368632)
Expected outcome: HeLa cells are successfully frozen and stored in liquid nitrogen.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately immerse in a 37°C water bath. Gently agitate until ice is completely melted (approximately 2-3 min).
Equipment: 37°C water bath (Thermo Scientific, 1000D)
Expected outcome: Cryovials are thawed completely without overheating.

DAY 1 | 40 min | Recovery Phase
Transfer thawed HeLa cells to a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL of complete DMEM. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to remove cryoprotectant. Resuspend cells in 1 mL of complete DMEM and incubate at 37°C for 30 min.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), 15 mL conical tubes
Expected outcome: Cells are recovered and resuspended, ready for viability assessment.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Mix 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL of trypan blue solution (0.4% w/v, Sigma-Aldrich, T8154) and load onto a hemocytometer. Count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells under a microscope (Olympus, CKX53) at 10x magnification.
Equipment: Hemocytometer (Hausser Scientific), Microscope (Olympus, CKX53)
Reagents: Trypan blue solution (0.4% w/v, Sigma-Aldrich, T8154)
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is determined based on cell counts.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate post-thaw viability percentage using the formula: (number of viable cells / total cells) × 100. Compare the viability percentages between the trehalose and DMSO groups using a two-tailed t-test with a significance level of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical analysis yields p-value for comparison of viability between groups.

DAY 1 | 2 days | Replication
Repeat the entire experiment (steps 1-8) two additional times to ensure replicability of results. Each replicate should follow the same protocol and conditions as outlined.
Expected outcome: Three independent replicates of the experiment are completed.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Documentation
Document all experimental conditions, observations, and results in a lab notebook. Ensure all data is recorded accurately for future reference and reproducibility.
Expected outcome: Complete and accurate records of the experiment are maintained.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Safety Review
Conduct a safety review of all materials used, ensuring proper disposal of biological waste and adherence to safety protocols regarding DMSO and cell culture materials.
Expected outcome: All safety protocols are followed, and waste is disposed of correctly.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose enhances post-thaw viability in HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
The freezing and thawing protocols are optimized for HeLa cells.
Cell viability can be accurately assessed using trypan blue exclusion.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability in trehalose group is significantly greater than 70%.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 between trehalose and DMSO groups.
Proceed if all three replicates yield consistent viability results.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of FBS for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (less than 10 passages).
Calibrate the centrifuge before each use to ensure consistent g-forces.

1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
